body:
Two original changes (provisioner + constitution-budget hook), plus a preserve-guard, plus an end-to-end sweep of the open issue backlog. All moving capabilities from "isn't available" / "written-only" to **utilized**.

---

## 1. Web-session toolchain provisioner (the headline) — `404fbec`

**Problem.** "isn't available in this session" — chezmoi, shellcheck, shfmt, yamllint, bats, gitleaks, just, pytest are all absent in Claude Code on the web, so `just lint` / `test` / `security` / `ci-local` can't run.

**Root cause (mapped, not guessed).** Every installer the repo relies on targets a host the remote network policy walls off (`apt`/`get.chezmoi.io`/`webinstall.dev`/`api.github.com`). But **PyPI** and **GitHub release downloads** are reachable, and that's sufficient:
- `chezmoi`, `shfmt`, `just`, `gitleaks`, `bats` → GitHub release binaries (pinned)
- `shellcheck` (via `shellcheck-py`), `yamllint`, `pytest` → PyPI

`.claude/hooks/session-start.sh` is remote-only (`CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE`), idempotent, arch-aware, persists PATH via `$CLAUDE_ENV_FILE`. **Validated:** full run 9s/exit 0 (8 tools), re-run 0.58s, `tests/test-templates.sh` → 96/0.

## 2. `constitution-budget` gate wired into hooks — `9d85313`

SessionStart surfaces the verdict warn-only; PostToolUse re-checks on `*CLAUDE.md`/`.tmpl` edits. Closes the CLAUDE.md task-queue item.

## 3. Preserve-guard — `af68451`

PreToolUse Bash guard that fires on deletion vectors (`rm` as a word, `sudo rm`, `&& rm`, `git rm`, `git clean -`) and injects the archive/disable/breadcrumb protocol — the mechanical end of "nothing gets deleted." Pattern verified precise (fires on real deletes, quiet on `npm`/`confirm`/`chmod`).

---

## 4. Open-issue sweep (explore → build → verify, grounded against current state)

Worked the open backlog. Six issues got verified code (all in-container: `bats`/`pytest`/`shellcheck`/`shfmt`):

Closes #7 — startup-budget assertion now BATS-covered (fires above budget, passes below; manifest-driven).
Closes #10 — `1PASSWORD_SETUP.md` gained the full secrets architecture (op→cache→env→consumers, TTY guard, gh-auth canonical, MCP hydration, AWS conditional). `doc-lint` green.
Closes #12 — `chezmoi-daemon` hardened: JSON `health.json` + JSONL run-log + `domus_auto_enabled` kill-switch (functionally verified via mock chezmoi).
Closes #13 — Unicode/symlink/permission test coverage (45 passed/2 skip-on-root) + fixed a real `~/Downloads` isolation leak in `test-domus-sort.bats`. Surfaced 3 tool bugs as documented assertions (no silent behavior changes).
Closes #14 — `domus-shell-parity --check` mode + `[shell-parity]` check wired into `domus doctor`.
Closes #20 — `domus doctor` now validates `AGENTS_*` paths (exist & writable) when the env contract is loaded.

**Verified:** shellcheck `-x` clean, shfmt clean, 36/36 `domus-cli` bats, shell-parity bats green, pytest 45/2, sort bats green.

### Disposition of the rest (commented on each thread)
- **#15 home-guard, #19 skills, #24 drift-monitoring** — already satisfied by the current on-demand (post-LaunchAgent) design; assessed + recommended close on-thread.
- **#18 context-sync** — orphaned (its plist was removed 2026-04-22); flagged on-thread, not deleted (preserve rule).
- **#21 bootstrap, #22 recovery** — require a live macOS VM / disaster sim; #22 is partly advanced by #10's secrets doc + existing gitleaks.
- **#31 resolver** — deferred by design (gated on GH 1:1 parity); left untouched.
- **#86 idea-debt, #87/#89 CI billing, #96 memory-audit** — need live-machine reach / account-level billing fix / are scheduled read-only; already correctly diagnosed (CI root cause = Actions spending limit, `runner_id:0`, 0 billable ms).

---

## CI status — pre-existing infra, not this PR

The red lint checks fail **on `master` too** and die in ~3s with `runner_id:0` / 0 billable ms — GitHub Actions never assigns a runner. Verified account-level (Actions spending limit / billing), not code. See #87/#89.

## Stage-honest status

The provisioner becomes *utilized* only once merged to `master` and a fresh web session runs its SessionStart hook. The daemon/bootstrap items remain verify-limited for the live macOS machine. Everything else is verified in-container as noted.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01R8hBqntdSyburTbztRuQ5e
title: Self-sufficient genome: toolchain provisioner, hook wiring, preserve-guard + open-issue sweep

body:
## What this is

The complete "guitar pedalboard for text & clipboard" — clean-room research synthesis **and a working end-to-end build, root to leaf.** Every artifact verified locally (CI is down account-side; see PR #83).

> Everything is a `stdin → stdout` filter (*a pedal*). The clipboard is the I/O jack, fzf is the patch bay, Espanso is the footswitch, markdown+SQLite+Notion is the recorder. Pedals are pure functions, so they chain in any order.

## Shipped

**Phase 0 — research synthesis** → `docs/PEDALBOARD.md` (6 cited angles; Maccy-over-Paste, capture-once/project-many, two-tier governance).

**Phase 1 — the pedalboard** → `pedal` runner + 15 atomic filters + `pedal pick` fzf patch bay.
```
printf 'Hello World' | pedal trim slugify      # chain, any order
pedal clip b64-decode json-pretty              # over the clipboard
```

**Phase 2 — Espanso footswitches (the "never type" layer)** → `private_Library/.../espanso/match/*.yml`.
`;j ;jm ;b64 ;jwt ;slug ;url ;rgb ;sha` fire pedal transforms over the clipboard and paste inline; `;b64j` chains two pedals; `;keep` captures the clipboard into `brain`; `;now ;today ;iso ;trim` utilities; `;email ;name ;gh` templated from chezmoi data. `cask "espanso"` added.

**Phase 3 — `domus clip` (intake & governance)** → `executable_domus-clip`, schema-introspecting Maccy reader. `status` / `search` / `govern [--apply]` / `keep`. Governance = regex + Shannon-entropy secret scan + SHA-256 dedup, **dry-run by default**, deletion refused unless the text column resolves. Wired into `domus`.

**Phase 4 — `brain` (capture once / project many)** → `executable_brain`. `add` (stdin → atomic markdown note, SHA-256 dedup), `index` (rebuild SQLite **FTS5**), `search`, `ls`, `push notion` (real stdlib API, rate-limited, env-gated). Markdown = truth, SQLite = rebuildable index, Notion = browse projection.

The whole chain composes: `pbpaste | pedal json-pretty | brain add --tags json` — and `;keep` does it from the keyboard.

## Verification (since CI can't run — see PR #83)

Compensating for the account-level Actions outage by running the repo's own linters locally on every artifact:
- `ruff` clean (brain, domus-clip); `shellcheck` + `shfmt -i 2 -ci` clean (pedal, pedals, edited `domus`); `yamllint` clean (espanso yml); `py_compile` OK.
- Functional end-to-end: brain add/dedup/index/FTS-search; domus-clip `govern` flags a planted AWS key + GitHub token + exact duplicate, dry-run vs `--apply` deletes 3 rows, `keep → brain` round-trips.

## Notes / remaining

- True *pre-persistence* secret gating needs a custom pasteboard watcher (Maccy auto-records); current `govern` is the post-write scrub documented in the architecture. TTL/max-size prune and one-time Paste.app import are the remaining Phase 3 leaves.
- `brain push notion` is real but unverified end-to-end here (needs your `NOTION_TOKEN`/`NOTION_DB`).

Draft. Content is green-verified locally; **CI red is the account-level Actions issue tracked in #83**, not this code.
title: The Pedalboard — full build (Phases 1–4): pedals, espanso footswitches, brain, domus clip

body:
## What

Instantiates a standing conductor directive into the genome so it **exists durably** rather than living only in a chat session ("if it doesn't do, it doesn't exist").

The directive: I run every automatable step myself (the human is conductor, not operator); the **only** legitimate hand-off is the irreducible residue that physically can't be automated — and *that* residue must be copy-pasteable.

## Why

This session demonstrated the model defaulting wrong twice — dumping command lists / asking permission instead of running things — which contradicts the existing `feedback_never_defer_commands.md` precedent and Rule #4 (Conductor principle). The directive refines that precedent by specifying the **form** of the residue, and the wrong-default is exactly the signal Rule #13 uses to justify a Tier-0 carrier.

## Changes

- **`reliquary/accumulated-rules-archive.md`** — new **Rule 67** (communication cluster): full text + provenance. Mandatory form for an irreducible hand-off:
  - exact run-as-is terminal commands with **dynamic vars** (`$DOMUS_ROOT`/`$HOME`/`$(chezmoi source-path)`) — never hardcoded paths or `<placeholders>` (mirrors the no-stubs default + the `run_before_validate-no-hardcoded-paths` apply-gate)
  - numbered verified steps with expected post-state
  - verified-live links (Rule #12 applied to URLs)
  - cross-reference `[[never-defer-commands]]` (67) added
- **`private_dot_claude/CLAUDE.md.tmpl`** (Tier-0 liturgy) — one standing-default bullet carrying Rule 67 every turn; pointer bumped `1-66` → `1-67`.
- **`reliquary/README.md`** — reconciled stale rule count (`1-63` → `1-67`).

## Placement rationale (Rule #13)

Durable home = Reliquary; Tier-0 carrier added because the rule changes a default the model gets wrong AND is not hook-enforced (no hook detects a deferral). Nothing deleted; additive only (Rule #3).

## Validation

- Edits are pure prose — no new chezmoi template directives; the single pre-existing `{{ }}` pair in `CLAUDE.md.tmpl` remains balanced.
- `chezmoi execute-template` not run here (chezmoi isn't installed in the remote clone); no render risk introduced since no template syntax was added.

> Note: `constitution-budget` gate (run before shipping Tier-0 edits) requires the local organvm toolchain — recommend running `just check-all` / the budget gate on a machine where chezmoi + organvm are present before merge.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Xw99HJLAxwWkKug6wdt4tT

title: Codify Rule 67: conductor hand-off form (run everything; irreducible residue is copy-pasteable)

body:
Closes #10.

## Problem
`1PASSWORD_SETUP.md` was a *testing quick-start* — it never documented the architecture, so it failed #10's gate: **"a new contributor can understand the full secrets flow from docs alone."**

## What this adds
An authoritative **Architecture** section, grounded line-by-line in the actual code (not assumptions), describing the three independent credential planes:

| Plane | Source of truth | Lifetime | File |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 — shell cache | `op read` → `~/.cache/op-secrets` (600, 60-min TTL, TTY-only bg refresh, TM-excluded) | per-shell, cached | `dot_config/private_op/secrets.zsh` |
| 2 — GitHub | `gh auth` (macOS Keychain) → `GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` | per-shell, live | `secrets.zsh` (+ never-export-`GITHUB_TOKEN` rule) |
| 3 — apply-time | chezmoi `onepasswordRead`/`onepasswordItemFields` | per `chezmoi apply`, never cached | AWS (`has_aws_credentials` gated), Claude/Cursor/Continue MCP (`is_ci` guarded), git SSH signing |

Plus: an ASCII flow diagram, a **cached-vs-live** table, the Plane-1 **threat model** summary, the `op-refresh` parity caveat, and the **disaster-recovery property** (ties into #21/#22). The original quick-start is preserved verbatim as "Setup & Testing".

## Validation
- Pure docs (`+102 −2`, one file). No secret *values* added — only `op://` reference paths and env-var names (manually scanned).
- Every claim cross-checked against `secrets.zsh`, `40-functions.zsh`, `20-tools.zsh`, `private_aws/credentials.tmpl`, the MCP templates, and `.chezmoiignore`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code/session_01Xw99HJLAxwWkKug6wdt4tT)_
title: docs(secrets): document the three-plane secrets architecture (closes #10)

body:
Walks **all 17 open issues** root-to-leaf (explore→plan→build→verify→route) and delivers the sandbox-tractable ones for real, routing the live-host/infra-blocked remainder to the authority. Full disposition: `docs/audit/2026-06-17-open-issues-triage.md`.

**Honors issue-closure governance** — nothing is closed. Each issue stays open until durable proof or a human decision; this PR links work and reports honest status.

## Delivered (verified in-sandbox: `bash -n` rendered, JSON re-validated, logic)

- **#10 (β.7) Secrets architecture docs** — `1PASSWORD_SETUP.md` gains a Secrets Architecture section: three credential paths (apply-time / shell-runtime / GitHub), the `~/.cache/op-secrets` cache + threat model, `gh auth`→`GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN` (never `GITHUB_TOKEN`/`GH_TOKEN`), MCP self-sourcing, rotation. Grounded in `secrets.zsh`.
- **#12 (γ.2) Self-heal daemon hardening** — `domus-daemon` **and** `chezmoi-daemon` now genuinely enforce `domus_auto_enabled` (skip the autonomous run when disabled). `docs/DAEMONS.md` claimed all daemons respected the flag, but only `domus doctor`'s status output did — the daemons ran regardless. Also writes `~/.local/state/domus/daemon-status.json`.
- **#20 (δ.5) Agent workspace env vars** — `domus doctor` `agents` check: `AGENTS_ROOT/BIN/CACHE/STATE/LOG` exist + writable.
- **#19 (δ.4) Skill pipeline** — `domus doctor` `skills` check: fails on dangling `~/.claude/skills`, warns if empty, reports linked count.

## Partial / designed (honest — not half-built blind)
- **#24 (ω.4) Drift monitoring** — `daemon-status.json` at-a-glance health seeded; the dedicated `domus drift` command + history is designed, not built this turn.
- **#14 (γ.4) Shell parity** — needs a `--check` exit mode + canonical-env assertion + `doctor` wiring.

## Routed to authority (live-host or infra blockers — Rule #67, not abandoned)
- **#87 ≡ #89** same root: CI runner/provisioning broken in the execution environment (dependency-free JSON job fails in ~3s; logs 404; all 33 JSON valid locally). Infra owner only.
- **#7 / #15 / #21 / #22 / #86** — need the live machine / `$HOME` / clean VM the sandbox can't reach.
- **#31** — gated on the GH-parity initiative by explicit design (IRF-OPS-045).
- **#18** — stale reference corrected: `registry-v2.json` → `repo-registry.json`; LaunchAgents axis no longer stale (now permitted under the bounded-contract rule).
- **#96** — automated rolling audit; wants `session-meta` in allowed repos.

## Verification ceiling (stated, not glossed)
No `chezmoi`/`shellcheck`/`bats`/live `$HOME` in this container. Shell verified via `bash -n` on rendered templates; JSON re-validated; the new `domus doctor` checks compose into the existing `_doctor_check` framework. Gates requiring a live `domus doctor` / `chezmoi apply` / clean-VM run are marked **pending live verification** in the triage — not claimed done.

body:
## Summary

- **PostCSS advisory (GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93, moderate XSS):** `next@16` bundles `postcss@8.4.31`. Added a `pnpm-workspace.yaml` override to force `postcss@>=8.5.10` workspace-wide; lockfile now resolves `8.5.15`. `pnpm audit` reports no known vulnerabilities.
- **System-path guard in `normalize-names`:** Rejects `--root /`, `/usr`, `/etc`, `/System`, `/Library`, `/Applications` (and their macOS `/private/*` symlink targets) with a clear error before any rename walk, preventing accidental mass-rename of OS files.
- **Stdin size cap in `bench-sanitize`:** Both `--mode request` and `--mode response` now refuse payloads larger than 32 MiB, guarding against runaway memory from oversized inputs.
- **`.gitignore`:** Added `node_modules/` which was missing for the `apps/web` workspace.
- **Tests:** `TestProtectedPathGuard` (6 cases in `test_normalize_names.py`) and new `test_bench_sanitize.py` (6 cases covering valid JSON, invalid JSON, oversized request, and oversized response). All 200 pytest cases pass.

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm audit` → no known vulnerabilities
- [ ] `python3 -m pytest tests/ -q` → 200 passed
- [ ] `ruff check --select E,F,W dot_local/bin/executable_{bench-sanitize,normalize-names} tests/test_bench_sanitize.py` → no errors
- [ ] CI: shellcheck, shfmt, yamllint, JSON validation, bats, pytest, gitleaks

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: security: pin postcss ≥8.5.10, guard system paths, bound bench-sanitize stdin

body:
## Summary

- **CVE-2026-41305 (postcss XSS, CVSS 6.1)**: Pin `postcss>=8.5.10` via `pnpm-workspace.yaml` overrides. The vulnerable `postcss@8.4.31` was a transitive dependency of `next`; it now resolves to `8.5.15`. `pnpm audit` is clean.
- **Lockfile**: Commit `pnpm-lock.yaml` to pin all 61 transitive Node.js deps to known-good versions and enable reproducible installs.
- **`just security` audit step**: Add `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` to the security recipe so future high-severity advisories surface automatically.
- **Input validation in `rules/prove.py`**: Validate the optional file-path argument — reject non-existent paths and non-`.json` extensions with exit code 2 before any file I/O.

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm audit` → "No known vulnerabilities found"
- [ ] `just security` runs gitleaks, permission check, and pnpm audit without errors
- [ ] `python3 rules/prove.py` → runs against default `rules.json`, all 67 rules pass
- [ ] `python3 rules/prove.py /etc/passwd` → exits 2 with "expected a .json file" error
- [ ] `python3 rules/prove.py /nonexistent.json` → exits 2 with "not a readable file" error
- [ ] `python3 -m pytest tests/ -q` → 188 passed

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: security: harden deps and add input validation

- **File permissions**: `chmod +x` 56 `executable_*` scripts in `dot_local/bin/` that were missing the execute bit, causing `just security` to fail
- **Dependency advisory**: Add `postcss: >=8.5.10` override in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` to resolve GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 (PostCSS XSS via unescaped `</style>`); lockfile updated with postcss 8.5.15
- **Input validation**: Guard `normalize-names --root` against system paths (`/`, `/etc`, `/usr`, `/bin`, `/sbin`, `/lib`, `/System`, `/Library`) with two new CLI tests

## Ecosystem audits

- `pip-audit`: no known vulnerabilities (7 internal packages skipped — not on PyPI)
- `pnpm audit`: 0 vulnerabilities after postcss override

body:
## Summary
- preserve the useful parts of the expanded CI draft on current `origin/master`
- add workflow `permissions`, concurrency cancellation, shared Python version config, zsh in the Ubuntu dependency set, and retry-hardened installer fetches
- add macOS apply/doctor coverage for the dotfiles render path
- split `fmt-check` from mutating `fmt` so CI checks formatting without rewriting the checkout
- apply the existing `shfmt` drift in `dot_local/bin/executable_domus-auth` required by the new check-only path

## Verification
- `ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/ci.yml"); puts "yaml ok"'`\n- `git diff --check origin/master..HEAD`\n- `just --dry-run check-all` shows `shfmt -d` and no `shfmt -w`\n- `just fmt-check`\n\n## Known blocker\n- Full local `just check-all` is red on pre-existing tests unrelated to this CI change: Brewfile manifest drift (`block-goose-cli`), `tests/../lint_test.sh` still using `#!/bin/bash`, and several `test-domus-desktop-router.bats` routing cases. I left those as separate lifecycle debt rather than mixing them into this CI hardening PR.\n
title: ci: harden domus workflow coverage

body:
## Summary

- **ci.yml**: Add `zsh` to `apt-get install` — bats tests 37–47 (`test-chezmoi-health.bats`) all run the full `chezmoi-health` script which checks for `zsh` as a required dependency via `check_dependencies`; the lint.yml BATS job already installed zsh but ci.yml's monolithic job did not, so those tests exited 2 (error) instead of 0
- **lint_test.sh**: Change `#!/bin/bash` → `#!/usr/bin/env bash` — bats test 63 ("no shell scripts use #!/bin/bash") was flagging it
- **executable_domus-auth**: Apply `shfmt -i 2 -ci` formatting (redirect spacing, trailing whitespace on blank line, case pattern spacing) — lint.yml Shell Formatting job was failing with `shfmt -d` diff
- **lint.yml line 89**: Wrap the 208-char `shfmt find` command across three lines — lint.yml YAML Lint job was failing with `[line-length] line too long (208 > 200 characters)`

## Test plan

body:
## Summary

Security hardening pass for domus-genoma addressing the GEN-organvm-domus-genoma-security-0628 task.

### Changes

**npm audit fix:**
- Added pnpm override to pin `postcss` to `^8.5.10` fixing GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 (moderate XSS via unescaped `</style>`)
- Zero vulnerabilities remain after fix
- `pnpm-lock.yaml` regenerated

**Input validation at untrusted entrypoints:**

| Route | Changes |
|-------|---------|
| `POST\|GET /api/install-script` | `MAX_EMAIL_LENGTH` (254), `MAX_TIER_LENGTH` (32), `MAX_BODY_BYTES` (4096) guards; `isValidTier()` length check |
| `GET\|POST /api/verify-license` | `MAX_TOKEN_LENGTH` (4096) and `MAX_REQUIRE_LENGTH` (32) bounds |
| `POST /api/webhooks/stripe` | `MAX_WEBHOOK_BODY_BYTES` (65536) returning 413; `MAX_EVENT_TYPE_LENGTH` (64) |

**Security headers:**
- Added Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options (nosniff), X-Frame-Options (DENY), Referrer-Policy via Next.js config

### Verification
- All 40 server integration tests pass
- `pnpm audit` reports zero vulnerabilities
title: security: hardening pass — pin postcss, input validation, CSP headers

body:
## Summary

- **YAML lint**: `.github/workflows/lint.yml` line 89 exceeded the 200-char limit (208 chars); split the `find … -exec` across lines
- **Shell formatting**: `dot_local/bin/executable_domus-auth` had three `shfmt` violations — redirect spacing (`> "$f"` → `>"$f"`), trailing whitespace, and `help|*` → `help | *` case-pattern spacing
- **Shebang test**: `lint_test.sh` used `#!/bin/bash`; the test suite (`test-chezmoi-scripts.bats`) requires `#!/usr/bin/env bash` for all scripts
- **chezmoi-health BATS tests**: `zsh` is not installed on ubuntu-24.04 CI runners; `check_dependencies` returns exit 2 when it can't find `zsh`, causing all 12 happy-path tests to fail. Fixed by adding a `zsh` mock to `setup()` in `test-chezmoi-health.bats` (matching the pattern already used in the explicit `check_dependencies` test case)

## Test plan

- [ ] CI Lint & Validate — YAML Lint and Shell Formatting jobs pass
- [ ] CI Build, Test, and Lint — BATS tests 36–47 (chezmoi-health) and test 63 (shebang check) pass

body:
## What

v2 corrections to `private_dot_claude/plans/2026-06-17-github-fullstack-strategy.md`, applying seven validated findings from the Codex review of #99 (which merged before the review landed).

| Finding | Fix |
|---|---|
| **P1 — unsafe public flip** | The repo tracks extensive private material (`private_dot_claude/`, `private_Documents/…1password-secret-coverage.md`). "Flip public" as a CI unblock would expose it — now gated on a disclosure scrub, with gating the macOS jobs as the preferred unblock. Fixed in Phase 0, Path A, Path D, §9. |
| Reversed quota economics | Per-org minutes don't pool, so 10 orgs = 10 separate allowances; consolidating *collapses* to one quota and can raise overage. Reframed: consolidation is for admin/security simplicity, **not** free minutes. |
| macOS runner | The failing jobs are `macos-14`; a Linux VPS can't serve them — require a macOS self-hosted runner or split/gate. |
| Runner visibility | Under Persona Model 2, `domus-genoma` stays personal → org runner groups can't reach it; need a repo-level runner or transfer-first. |
| Transfer preflight | Dedupe shell-repo names (`.github`/`github.io`) before batch transfer or it strands mid-run. |
| Pages migration | Transfer redirects don't cover GitHub Pages — re-publish + update DNS before retiring source orgs. |
| Billing not UI-only | The 2026 billing REST APIs expose usage/budgets; only downgrades/cancellations need the UI. |

Docs-only. CI red remains the known account-level Actions runner cap, not this change.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

body:
Closes #20.

## Problem
`15-env.zsh.tmpl` exports `AGENTS_ROOT/BIN/CACHE/STATE/LOG`, and `run_once_after_create-agents-dirs.sh.tmpl` provisions them — but **nothing verified they exist/are writable**. #20's gate: *"`domus doctor` verifies all AGENTS_* paths exist and are writable."* There was no check and no test.

## Change
Adds an `agents` check to `cmd_doctor` (JSON-mode aware via the existing `_doctor_check` helper):
- Resolves `AGENTS_ROOT` from the exported env, falling back to `$repo_root/_agents` so it works outside an agent shell.
- Verifies all five paths (`root + bin/cache/state/log`), honoring `AGENTS_BIN/CACHE/STATE/LOG` overrides independently.
- Reports: `ok` (5/5 present+writable) · `warn` missing (with the exact `mkdir -p …/{bin,cache,state,log}` remediation, sets `exit_code=1`) · `warn` not-writable · `skip` (root unresolved).

## Verification (in-container)
- `tests/test-domus-doctor-agents.bats` — 4 cases: ok / missing / unwritable (root-guarded) / independent `AGENTS_STATE` override. **4/4 pass** (unwritable skips under root, runs on CI's non-root runner).
- No regression: `tests/test-domus-cli.bats` **29/29** green.
- `shellcheck -e SC2317,SC1128,SC2015 -x` clean · `shfmt -d -i 2 -ci` clean (the two CI lint jobs' exact commands).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

body:
Closes #13.

## Two-part gate
**Part 1 — "all file-automation tests pass in CI; no test touches real `~/Desktop`|`~/Downloads`"** — already met, re-verified:
- bats tests isolate via `$HOME`→`mktemp` redirect (`setup_test_env`); pytest via `tempfile`/`TemporaryDirectory`.
- The `~/Desktop` / `~/Downloads` strings in `test_routing_law.py` are **manifest-YAML assertions** (config-parity), not filesystem ops.
- Both `bats` and `pytest` run in CI (`.github/workflows/lint.yml`).

**Part 2 — explicit edge cases (Unicode filenames, symlinks, permissions)** — had **zero** coverage. This PR adds it.

## Added (assertions pinned to verified real behavior)
- `test_normalize_names.py::TestUnicodeFilenames` — accents/CJK/emoji/Greek preserved and lowercased Unicode-aware: `Café Münü.PDF → café-münü.pdf`, `日本語 ドキュメント.TXT → 日本語-ドキュメント.txt`, `Ω_Ψ.MD → ω-ψ.md`, emoji preserved. Guards against mojibake/data-loss.
- `test_downloads_tidy.py::TestSymlinkAndPermissionEdgeCases` — the shared `unique_dest` collision resolver (backs **sort + downloads-tidy + naming**) against: valid symlink (`link-1.txt`), multi-ext symlink (`link-1.tar.gz`), broken/dangling symlink (returned as-is — pins actual behavior), and read-only file (`ro-1.txt`).

## Verification (in-container)
- Full pytest suite **138 passed** (was 128).
- Added lines ruff-clean. The one pre-existing E501 in `test_downloads_tidy.py` is untouched and unrelated (CI's ruff job scopes to `dot_local/bin/executable_*`, not `tests/`).

body:
Closes #14.

## Problem
`domus-shell-parity` reported zsh/fish drift but **always exited 0**, so the signal wasn't machine-consumable and `domus doctor` couldn't surface it. #14's gate: *"`domus shell-parity` reports clean or identifies actionable drift"* + "integrate with `domus doctor`."

## Change
- **`executable_domus-shell-parity.tmpl`** — add a `PARITY_DRIFT` accumulator that `diff_sets` flips when either shell has entries the other lacks; the script now **exits 0 (clean) / 1 (drift)**. `--help` still exits 0.
- **`executable_domus` (`cmd_doctor`)** — new `shell-parity` check: runs the tool quietly and maps `exit 0 → ok`, `exit 1 → info`, absent → `skip`. Deliberately **`info`, not `warn`** — zsh/fish divergence is often intentional (zsh-specific tooling: the repo currently shows 57 zsh-only aliases), so it's surfaced without failing doctor's exit code or the macOS **Doctor Check** CI job.

## Verification (in-container, since the tool reads config files, not a live fish)
- Tool exits **1** on the real repo (drift present) and **0** on a clean fixture; `--help` → 0.
- `tests/test-domus-doctor-shell-parity.bats` — ok / info / skip paths, **3/3 pass**.
- `test-domus-cli.bats` **29/29** (no regression); `executable_domus` shellcheck + shfmt clean.
- The tool file is `*.tmpl` (not `*.sh.tmpl`), so it sits outside both CI shellcheck globs by design; the one `executable_domus` edit that *is* linted is clean.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code/session_01Xw99HJLAxwWkKug6wdt4tT)_
title: feat(doctor): shell-parity check + exit-code contract (closes #14)

body:
Addresses #7 (partial — the container-buildable, verifiable slice).

## Problem
`domus perf shell` showed the last-10 trend but computed **no median** and had **no threshold gate**, so #7's two mechanizable asks — *"reports ≤100ms median"* and *"add startup-time assertion to CI (warn if >150ms)"* — had nothing to hang on.

## Change
- **`cmd_perf_shell`** — compute + display the **median** of the last 10 samples (bash-3.2-safe: no `mapfile`; `pipefail`-guarded for empty samples).
- **`domus perf shell --assert [ms]`** — exits **0** if median ≤ threshold, **1** if over, **2** if no data. Threshold precedence: explicit arg > manifest `target_ms` > built-in 400. Drop this into CI / a pre-commit hook as the regression gate.
- Help text documents `--assert`.

## Verification (in-container, synthetic telemetry)
- Median odd/even/single → 90 / 25 / 77; assert pass/fail/no-data → exit 0 / 1 / 2; default-threshold path.
- `tests/test-domus-perf-shell-assert.bats` **6/6**; `test-domus-cli.bats` **29/29** (no regression); shellcheck + shfmt clean.

## Residue (needs the live machine — not this container)
The actual **≤100ms measurement** and **deferring slow tool inits** (starship/zoxide/atuin via `_cache.zsh`) both require profiling a real interactive zsh. This PR ships the **measurement + gate mechanism** those will be judged against; the profiling/optimization is the remaining live-machine work for #7's final gate. Suggest keeping #7 open until that lands.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

body:
Refs organvm/universal-mail--automation#154 and organvm/universal-mail--automation#155.

## Summary
- replace the chezmoi mail-triage template with a UMA status wrapper
- make `--apply` refuse from Domus so apply only means gated UMA mailbox mutation
- update tests to assert Domus contains no independent classifier symbols

## Verification
- `python3 -m py_compile dot_local/bin/executable_mail-triage.tmpl`
- `python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_mail_triage.py`
- UMA union guard against clean Limen/Domus wrapper worktrees: 247 scanned files, 0 violations
title: Mail: wrap triage with UMA status
